title: Racosperma costatum
taxonomy: Racosperma costatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the subgenus Acacia. The subgenus Acacia is further divided into various sections, with Racosperma costatum belonging to one of them. This plant's classification is part of ongoing research in botanical taxonomy. Further studies may refine its classification within the Acacia subgenus.
uses: Racosperma costatum, also known as the black wattle, has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds.
